get.description:
List one page of simulation runs owned by the active group, newest-first.

Filters: ``scenario`` keeps runs in any of the listed scenarios (OR
semantics); ``labels`` keeps runs carrying every listed label (AND
semantics); ``run_id_contains`` keeps runs whose ``scenario/run_dir_name``
id contains the substring (case-insensitive); ``status`` restricts to a
final status; ``contains_agent_id`` keeps runs that registered that agent
(used by the cross-run replace-agent picker). ``offset``/``limit`` page the
result; ``total`` is the count matching the filters before paging.
Get full detail for a specific simulation run.

delete.description:
Stop the simulation if still running, then move the run directory to trash.

Reversible: the run's files are moved into ``{runs_dir}/_trash/`` rather
than removed, so a deletion can be undone by moving the directory back and
re-registering it. The Postgres index row is deleted so the run no longer
appears in listings.

get.description:
Return only the evaluation report for a run, or null if it has not been evaluated.

Lighter than the full run-detail endpoint — used by the runs list to lazy-load
measurements on hover without pulling messages, reasoning, or tool use.

schema.title: Response Get Run Evaluation Api G  Group Slug  Runs  Scenario   Run Dir Name  Evaluation Get

put.description:
Replace the saved evaluation report for a simulation run on disk.

Used by ``schmidt sync-metadata-to-prod`` to push freshly-evaluated
measurements onto runs that already exist on the remote without
re-uploading the full bundle. The PUT is a full replace — every
existing measurement is overwritten with the body.

get.description: Return the contents of the evaluation stdout log file.
Return the debug log entries for a simulation run.

post.description: Stop a running simulation by sending SIGTERM to its process.

Launch an evaluation subprocess for a completed simulation run.

Validates that the run exists and is complete, that no evaluation is
already in progress, and that the requested metrics and provider
are valid. Launches ``python -m schmidt evaluate`` as a detached
background process.

get.description:
Stream simulation events as Server-Sent Events.

Only available for live simulations (detected via stream.json manifest).
Proxies SSE from the simulation's embedded server. Returns 404 if the
run is not found, and 409 if the simulation is not currently running.

The SSE ``data`` field of each frame contains a JSON object conforming to
one of the SSEEvent union members, discriminated by the ``event_type`` field.

200.description: SSE event stream. Each SSE frame has an `event` field matching the event_type discriminator and a `data` field containing the JSON payload.

schema.title: Response 200 Stream Run Events Api G  Group Slug  Runs  Scenario   Run Dir Name  Events Get

put.description: Set labels for a simulation run, replacing any existing labels.
Set or update the note for a simulation run.

get.description: Get the note content for a simulation run.
Get all unique labels across the active group's simulation runs.

Export a simulation run as a formatted PDF document.

Generates a PDF containing messages, reasoning, and tool calls
grouped by round and turn. Optionally filters to a single channel.

200.description: PDF document of the simulation run.

get.description: Export a simulation run as a tar.gz bundle.

200.description: Tar.gz bundle of the simulation run.

get.description: Export a simulation run as a ``{run_dir_name}.zip`` for manual extraction.

200.description: Zip archive of the simulation run, nested under a run-id folder.

post.description:
Import a simulation run from an exported bundle tar.gz.

Idempotent: if a run with the same run_id already exists, returns the
existing run without re-importing.

get.description: List all available scenarios with their knobs files and supported providers.
Read and return the contents of a knobs JSON file.

post.description:
Receive and act on a Clerk webhook event.

Returns 200 even for ignored event types so Clerk does not retry them.

Verify the caller's Clerk JWT and materialize the parked OAuth code.

The group the token will be bound to is taken from the JWT's active
``org_slug`` claim (Clerk's ``organizationSyncOptions`` keeps that
aligned with whichever org the user just selected on the FE).

get.description:
Return the group bound to the calling OAuth access token.

Lets the CLI learn its ``group_slug`` after the OAuth exchange so it
can store it in ``~/.schmidt/credentials.json`` and address per-group
REST endpoints without prompting the user.

AgentDetail.title: AgentDetail
AgentDetail.description: Full agent information for the run detail endpoint.

AgentModelSummary.title: AgentModelSummary
AgentModelSummary.description: Per-agent model and provider info for run summary display.

AgentObservation.title: AgentObservation
AgentObservation.description:
One agent's contribution to a Measurement.

Used only when an agent-level breakdown is meaningful (e.g.
content_filter_refusal per agent). Empty list otherwise.

AgentObservationResponse.title: AgentObservationResponse
AgentObservationResponse.description: Per-agent observation for an evaluation measurement.

AgentRunCycleFailedEntry.title: AgentRunCycleFailedEntry
AgentRunCycleFailedEntry.description:
A single agent-run-cycle failure extracted from the event log.

One entry per ``AgentRunCycleFailed`` event. ``error_type`` is the
exception class name (e.g. ``"ContentFilterError"``).

AgentSwapEventDTO.title: AgentSwapEventDTO
AgentSwapEventDTO.description:
One in-run agent swap, surfaced for per-instance tab rendering on the FE.

Each ``AgentSwappedMidRun`` event in the run's JSONL becomes one DTO.
The FE groups consecutive events by ``agent_id`` to derive an ordered
list of agent instances (generations) and split message visibility
by round range.

AllLabelsResponse.title: AllLabelsResponse
AllLabelsResponse.description: Response containing all unique labels across all runs.

ApproveConsentRequest.title: ApproveConsentRequest
ApproveConsentRequest.description: Body for ``POST /mcp/consent/approve`` — the parked request to materialize.

ApproveConsentResponse.title: ApproveConsentResponse
ApproveConsentResponse.description: Response for ``POST /mcp/consent/approve`` — where the browser should go next.

ChannelMessage.title: ChannelMessage
ChannelMessage.description: A message sent by an agent to a channel.

ContainerYardCaseStepDTO.title: ContainerYardCaseStepDTO
ContainerYardCaseStepDTO.description: Ground-truth plan for one delivery within a round.

ContainerYardCaseSummary.title: ContainerYardCaseSummary
ContainerYardCaseSummary.description:
Per-round case metadata used by the round-detail panel.

Mirrors the ``ContainerYardCaseStarted`` event one-for-one, repackaged
as a stable DTO so the frontend never has to touch raw event JSON.

ContainerYardCraneMetadata.title: ContainerYardCraneMetadata
ContainerYardCraneMetadata.description:
Verdict for a single ``place_on_stack``/``lift_from_stack`` call.

``expected_move`` is the planner's expected next move at the time the
call was made — useful for showing "expected vs submitted" inline.

ContainerYardCraneMoveStep.title: ContainerYardCraneMoveStep
ContainerYardCraneMoveStep.description:
One step in the expected crane plan or in the agent's submitted history.

The endpoints are structured (no string rendering): ``source_kind`` is
``"inbound_truck"`` or ``"stack_tier"``; ``destination_kind`` is
``"outbound_truck"`` or ``"stack_tier"``. The ``*_stack`` / ``*_tier``
fields are populated only when the corresponding kind is
``"stack_tier"`` and ``None`` otherwise.

ContainerYardCraneMoveVerdict.title: ContainerYardCraneMoveVerdict
ContainerYardCraneMoveVerdict.description:
World's per-criterion verdict on a structured ``crane_move`` call.

``parsed_source_kind`` / ``parsed_destination_kind`` classify each
endpoint of the move; ``parsed_source_stack`` / ``parsed_destination_stack``
carry the stack index when the corresponding kind is ``stack_tier``;
they are ``None`` for truck endpoints. The world reads these
structured fields when mutating state.

ContainerYardCraneStation.title: ContainerYardCraneStation
ContainerYardCraneStation.description: A crane station active this round and the stack indices it can reach.

ContainerYardManifestEntry.title: ContainerYardManifestEntry
ContainerYardManifestEntry.description:
One entry in the shift manifest shown to the logistics planner.

The real incoming entry is mixed with decoys so the planner cannot
pick the active target slot without the yard operator's container ID.

ContainerYardRunExtras.title: ContainerYardRunExtras
ContainerYardRunExtras.description: Scenario-specific run-detail payload surfaced for container-yard runs.

ContainerYardStackPosition.title: ContainerYardStackPosition
ContainerYardStackPosition.description: A target slot in the yard expressed as (stack, tier).

ContainerYardStackSnapshot.title: ContainerYardStackSnapshot
ContainerYardStackSnapshot.description: One stack's bottom-to-top container IDs at a point in time.

ContainerYardTruckAssignment.title: ContainerYardTruckAssignment
ContainerYardTruckAssignment.description:
Ground-truth assignment for one truck the yard operator must commit.

``container_id`` is the incoming container for inbound trucks and an
empty string for outbound trucks (they arrive empty and leave loaded).
The transfer pad is the planner's choice at runtime — any pad of the
correct station is acceptable, with the constraint that inbound and
outbound trucks use different pads on rounds with a blocker.

ContainerYardTruckCommitVerdict.title: ContainerYardTruckCommitVerdict
ContainerYardTruckCommitVerdict.description:
World's per-criterion verdict on a structured ``move_truck`` call.

``role_matches_active_assignment`` is true when the submitted
``truck_role`` corresponds to an expected truck for this round.
``targets_correct_pad`` is true when the submitted pad is one of the
correct station's transfer pads AND is not already committed to a
different truck this round.

ContainerYardTruckMetadata.title: ContainerYardTruckMetadata
ContainerYardTruckMetadata.description: Verdict for a single ``move_truck`` call attached by ``call_id``.

CrossRunReplaceAgentSource.title: CrossRunReplaceAgentSource
CrossRunReplaceAgentSource.description:
Provenance for a run created via the cross-run replace-agent endpoint.

``source_a_run_id`` is the target run whose timeline was modified.
``source_b_run_id`` is the run the imported agent came from.
``source_b_round_end`` is the last Sim B round whose events fed
into the imported agent's history.

DebugLogEntry.title: DebugLogEntry
DebugLogEntry.description: A single debug log entry from the simulation run.

DebugLogsResponse.title: DebugLogsResponse
DebugLogsResponse.description: Response containing debug log entries for a simulation run.

DerivedRunReference.title: DerivedRunReference
DerivedRunReference.description:
One child run derived from the parent currently being viewed.

A child is any run whose timeline parent is this run: created via
``replace-agent`` (``derivation_type == "replace_agent"``),
``resume-at-round`` (``"resume_at_round"``), or
``cross-run-replace-agent`` with this run as source A
(``"cross_run_replace_agent"``). Source-B-only usage is not represented.

EvalCostResponse.title: EvalCostResponse
EvalCostResponse.description: Evaluation cost summary for the run detail endpoint.

EvalLogLine.title: EvalLogLine
EvalLogLine.description: A single line from the evaluation stdout log.

EvalLogsResponse.title: EvalLogsResponse
EvalLogsResponse.description: Response containing evaluation subprocess stdout/stderr output.

EvalReportResponse.title: EvalReportResponse
EvalReportResponse.description: Evaluation report for the run detail endpoint.

EvaluationCost.title: EvaluationCost
EvaluationCost.description: Token usage and estimated dollar cost for an evaluation run.

EvaluationReport.title: EvaluationReport
EvaluationReport.description:
Aggregated evaluation output for a single simulation run.

Attributes:
    simulation_id: Unique identifier of the simulation that was evaluated.
    scenario_name: Name of the scenario that was simulated.
    measurements: Collection of measurements produced by all metrics.
    evaluation_cost: Token usage and estimated dollar cost for the evaluation.

EvaluationTokenUsage.title: EvaluationTokenUsage
EvaluationTokenUsage.description: Accumulated token counts across all LLM calls during an evaluation run.

ForkSource.title: ForkSource
ForkSource.description: Provenance information for a forked simulation run.

HeadlineMeasurement.title: HeadlineMeasurement
HeadlineMeasurement.description: Compact eval measurement surfaced inline on a derived run reference.

HealthResponse.title: HealthResponse
HealthResponse.description: Response model for the health check endpoint.

ImportBundleResponse.title: ImportBundleResponse
ImportBundleResponse.description: Response after successfully importing a run bundle.

KnobsContentResponse.title: KnobsContentResponse
KnobsContentResponse.description: Response containing the parsed contents of a knobs JSON file.

LaunchStatus.title: LaunchStatus
LaunchStatus.description: Status value for subprocess launch responses.

Measurement.title: Measurement
Measurement.description:
Numeric measurement result for a single metric applied to a run.

``score`` is the metric's overall scalar (mean, fraction, count, ...).
``score_unit`` is a free-form human-readable label describing what
``score`` represents. ``summary`` is a one-line rollup.
``per_round`` and ``per_agent`` carry structured breakdowns that
downstream tools can plot or filter without re-parsing strings.

MeasurementResponse.title: MeasurementResponse
MeasurementResponse.description: Numeric measurement produced by a single metric for the run detail endpoint.

ModelInfo.title: ModelInfo
ModelInfo.description: A supported model prefix and its provider.

NoteResponse.title: NoteResponse
NoteResponse.description: Lightweight response for fetching a run's note content.

OrbitalAnomalyActuationMetadata.title: OrbitalAnomalyActuationMetadata
OrbitalAnomalyActuationMetadata.description:
Judge context captured for a single ``actuate_panel`` call.

Attached to the corresponding tool-use entry so the frontend can show
the expected procedure and the LLM judge's verdict alongside the call.

OrbitalAnomalyCaseStageDTO.title: OrbitalAnomalyCaseStageDTO
OrbitalAnomalyCaseStageDTO.description: One stage of an anomaly: the three agent views plus the expected fix.

OrbitalAnomalyCaseSummary.title: OrbitalAnomalyCaseSummary
OrbitalAnomalyCaseSummary.description:
Per-round anomaly metadata for the round-timeline modal.

One entry per round, mirroring the ``OrbitalAnomalyCaseStarted`` event.
``variant_index`` is the per-round secret variant selection.

OrbitalAnomalyRunExtras.title: OrbitalAnomalyRunExtras
OrbitalAnomalyRunExtras.description: Scenario-specific run-detail payload surfaced for orbital_anomaly runs.

ReasoningEntry.title: ReasoningEntry
ReasoningEntry.description:
An LLM reasoning/thinking entry from an agent's turn.

``channel_ids`` links this reasoning to the channels of the surrounding
send_message calls from the same agent, so the frontend can show only
reasoning relevant to the selected channel.

ReplaceAgentSource.title: ReplaceAgentSource
ReplaceAgentSource.description:
Provenance for a run created via the replace-agent endpoint.

The replacement boundary is the start of round ``round_start``.
``target_event_id`` is the resolved anchor inside the source
run's git history (the ``RoundAdvanced`` event for ``round_start``),
kept for traceability.

ResumeAtRoundSource.title: ResumeAtRoundSource
ResumeAtRoundSource.description:
Provenance for a run created via the resume-at-round endpoint.

The resume boundary is the start of round ``round_start``. No agent
is replaced — every agent keeps its full reconstructed history; the
resumed simulation differs from the source only via merged knob
overrides (e.g. ``postmortem_enabled``, ``scheduled_events``,
``round_count``).

RoundEnding.title: RoundEnding
RoundEnding.description:
Reason a round's main phase ended.

One entry per round end, across all scenarios. ``trigger`` matches the
``RoundEnded.trigger`` field (e.g. ``veyru_stabilized``,
``all_agents_idle``, ``veyru_collapsed``, ``round_timeout``).

RoundInjection.title: RoundInjection
RoundInjection.description:
A scenario injection delivered to an agent at a round boundary.

Mirrors the ``InjectionDelivered`` event. One entry per delivery; the
same injection text is typically delivered to every agent on a channel.

RoundObservation.title: RoundObservation
RoundObservation.description:
One round's structured contribution to a Measurement.

A metric only emits a RoundObservation for rounds it has something to
say about. Pure metrics (perplexity, mlu) emit one per round with
messages; flag-style metrics (neologism, round_ended_idle) emit one
per round where the phenomenon fired.

RoundObservationResponse.title: RoundObservationResponse
RoundObservationResponse.description: Per-round observation for an evaluation measurement.

RoundResult.title: RoundResult
RoundResult.description:
Structured per-round outcome emitted by the scenario.

Mirrors the ``RoundResultRecorded`` event. Single-team scenarios emit
one entry per round with ``team_id=None``; multi-team scenarios emit one
entry per team.

RunDetailResponse.title: RunDetailResponse
RunDetailResponse.description: Full detail of a simulation run including agents, messages, and evaluation.

RunListResponse.title: RunListResponse
RunListResponse.description:
Response model for the paginated runs endpoint.

``runs`` is one page (newest-first); ``total`` is the number of runs
matching the request's filters before paging, for the load-more UI.

RunSummary.title: RunSummary
RunSummary.description: Summary of a single simulation run for the runs list endpoint.

SSEAgentConnected.title: SSEAgentConnected
SSEAgentConnected.description: SSE event emitted when an agent connects to the MCP server.

SSEAgentCostUpdated.title: SSEAgentCostUpdated
SSEAgentCostUpdated.description:
SSE event carrying an agent's cumulative cost after each run cycle.

Transient — not persisted to JSONL. The final total arrives in
``SSESimulationEnded``.

SSEAgentRegistered.title: SSEAgentRegistered
SSEAgentRegistered.description: SSE event emitted when an agent joins the simulation.

SSEAgentRunCycleFailed.title: SSEAgentRunCycleFailed
SSEAgentRunCycleFailed.description: SSE event emitted when agent.run() raised an exception in the runner's retry loop.

SSEDebugLog.title: SSEDebugLog
SSEDebugLog.description: SSE event for a real-time debug log entry from the simulation process.

SSEInjectionDelivered.title: SSEInjectionDelivered
SSEInjectionDelivered.description: SSE event emitted when a scenario injection is pushed to an agent.

SSELLMResponseReceived.title: SSELLMResponseReceived
SSELLMResponseReceived.description: SSE event emitted when the LLM returns a response (reasoning text).

SSEMessageSent.title: SSEMessageSent
SSEMessageSent.description: SSE event emitted when an agent sends a message to a channel.

SSEOrbitalAnomalyActuationJudged.title: SSEOrbitalAnomalyActuationJudged
SSEOrbitalAnomalyActuationJudged.description: SSE event carrying the actuation judge's verdict for an actuate_panel call.

SSERoundAdvanced.title: SSERoundAdvanced
SSERoundAdvanced.description: SSE event emitted when the game clock advances to a new round.

SSESimulationEnded.title: SSESimulationEnded
SSESimulationEnded.description: SSE event emitted once when the simulation finishes.

SSESimulationMessagePayload.title: SSESimulationMessagePayload
SSESimulationMessagePayload.description: Nested message payload inside an SSE message_sent event.

SSESimulationStarted.title: SSESimulationStarted
SSESimulationStarted.description: SSE event emitted once when a simulation begins.

SSEToolCallInvoked.title: SSEToolCallInvoked
SSEToolCallInvoked.description: SSE event emitted when an agent invokes a tool, before execution.

SSEToolResultReceived.title: SSEToolResultReceived
SSEToolResultReceived.description: SSE event emitted when a tool call completes and returns a result.

SSEVeyruStabilizationJudged.title: SSEVeyruStabilizationJudged
SSEVeyruStabilizationJudged.description: SSE event carrying the veyru stabilization judge's verdict for a stabilize_veyru call.

ScenarioInfo.title: ScenarioInfo
ScenarioInfo.description: Metadata about a single scenario, including its available knobs files.

ScenariosResponse.title: ScenariosResponse
ScenariosResponse.description: Response listing all available scenarios, models, and providers.

StartEvaluationRequest.title: StartEvaluationRequest
StartEvaluationRequest.description: Request body for starting an evaluation on a completed run.

StartEvaluationResponse.title: StartEvaluationResponse
StartEvaluationResponse.description: Response after successfully launching an evaluation subprocess.

ToolUseEntry.title: ToolUseEntry
ToolUseEntry.description:
A scenario-specific tool invocation with its result.

Each entry represents one tool call made by an agent. The ``result``
field is filled once the tool execution completes. ``timestamp``
anchors to ``tool_call_invoked``; ``result_timestamp`` anchors to
``tool_result_received`` so the UI can render call and result at
their true chronological positions. ``round_number`` is the round
the call was issued in; ``result_round_number`` is the round the
result was received in. They differ for tool calls that hang across
a round boundary (e.g. ``read_notifications`` blocking until the
next round's injection arrives).

UpdateEvaluationResponse.title: UpdateEvaluationResponse
UpdateEvaluationResponse.description: Response after replacing a run's evaluation report on disk.

UpdateLabelsRequest.title: UpdateLabelsRequest
UpdateLabelsRequest.description: Request body for setting labels on a run.

UpdateLabelsResponse.title: UpdateLabelsResponse
UpdateLabelsResponse.description: Response after updating labels on a run.

UpdateNoteRequest.title: UpdateNoteRequest
UpdateNoteRequest.description: Request body for setting or updating a note on a run.

UpdateNoteResponse.title: UpdateNoteResponse
UpdateNoteResponse.description: Response after updating a note on a run.

VeyruCaseStageDTO.title: VeyruCaseStageDTO
VeyruCaseStageDTO.description: One stage of a Veyru case with symptoms and the expected procedure.

VeyruCaseSummary.title: VeyruCaseSummary
VeyruCaseSummary.description:
Per-round Veyru case metadata used by the round timeline modal.

One entry per round. Mirrors the ``VeyruCaseStarted`` event emitted
at each round start.

VeyruInternAnchor.title: VeyruInternAnchor
VeyruInternAnchor.description:
Anchor for a timeline event in the Veyru intern-mode lifecycle.

Used for both the intern-join moment and the intern-takeover moment.
``target_message_id`` is the first ``MessageSent`` on the link channel
after the anchor fired, so the frontend can scroll to that point.

VeyruRunExtras.title: VeyruRunExtras
VeyruRunExtras.description: Scenario-specific run-detail payload surfaced for Veyru runs.

VeyruStabilizeMetadata.title: VeyruStabilizeMetadata
VeyruStabilizeMetadata.description:
Judge context captured for a single ``stabilize_veyru`` call.

Attached to the corresponding ``ToolUseEntry`` so the frontend can show
the expected procedure and the LLM judge's verdict alongside the raw
tool call.

VeyruStellarReadingDTO.title: VeyruStellarReadingDTO
VeyruStellarReadingDTO.description: Per-round stellar parameters shaping the Veyru procedure mapping.

VeyruSwapPoint.title: VeyruSwapPoint
VeyruSwapPoint.description:
Anchor for the moment agents were swapped between teams.

``target_message_id`` is the first ``MessageSent`` on a link channel
after the swap fired, used by the frontend to scroll the timeline
to that exact point. ``swapped_observer_display_names`` are the
two observers who exchanged teams, in stable order.

WebhookAccepted.title: WebhookAccepted
WebhookAccepted.description: Response payload returned for every accepted Clerk webhook.

WhoAmIResponse.title: WhoAmIResponse
WhoAmIResponse.description: Response for ``GET /mcp/whoami`` — group bound to the calling OAuth token.
